text.title: mcp-resource-vs-tool-vs-prompt Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mcp-resource-vs-tool-vs-prompt as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Tool that always returns the same content (e.g., get_doc_index() returning a 50KB docs manifest). The tool is used like a Resource — the model "reads" it — but consumes a tool slot and bloats every turn the model decides to invoke it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This tool should be a Resource at a URI like docs://_index. Now the model only loads it when explicitly relevant, and it doesn't pollute the tool list on every turn.

failure-mode.description: Resource that mutates state when accessed (e.g., GET runbook://incident-foo starts the incident playbook in the background). Resources are supposed to be read-only; side effects make replay and audit impossible.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Convert to a Tool like start_runbook(name). Tools are designed for side effects; the model can reason about when to invoke them and what the consequences are.

failure-mode.description: Tool meant to be invoked by the user (e.g., a Tool called `initialize_project` that the team expects the user to run at startup). The Tool never gets called because the model doesn't know to invoke it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Create a Prompt like /initialize-project. Prompts are the user-facing interface; the user sees them in the slash menu and invokes them. The Prompt can internally call the Tool if needed.

failure-mode.description: Over-broad Tool with a mode argument (e.g., exec(mode="read"|"write"|"delete", target, ...). The model confuses the modes 25% of the time — it might write when it meant to read.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Split into N named tools: read_file(path), write_file(path, content), delete_file(path). Clear names let the model reason about the operation.
